text:
On a tranquil autumn evening, in a town called Willow Creek, there lived a girl named Lily who loved flying kites. Every year at this time, she would spend hours up in her backyard, attaching bright feathers to a large, elegant paper dragon. She would stand cautiously on the grass, make sure no shadows were lurking, and then launch her creation into the sky.

Lily's kites lived incredibly long lives. They’d soar into clouds, perform elegant spins, and return looking like fairies. Her dragon was no exception; after a few months, it would fall silent, then gradually return with a delicate rustle, having found paradise in some distant constellation.

One year, while she was prepping her favorite kite, Lily heard a faint buzzing. Peering through her lens, she spotted a bee, caught in its own silken net. The bee was covered in glittering gold flecks. Lily knew she had to help.

As gently as possible, she freed the bee.
